description: Strace: The absolute foundational process logging tool intercepting and aggressively monitoring deep root Linux System Calls (Syscalls) interfacing bridging standard programs across local Kernels.

commands.explanation: Executes explicitly bounded binaries dynamically mapping every single deeply hooked system call (like file reads, IP accesses) actively streaming it straight into live stdout logs.
Eliminates extreme tracking noise inherently restricting the execution pipeline logic strictly trapping ONLY specified systemic interactions.
Forcefully isolates deeply embedded logic matrices dynamically capturing any localized raw API requests executing explicitly attempting outbound global internet connections.
Attaches the aggressive monitoring layer onto a completely decoupled natively running background process exposing real-time explicit data-manipulations without termination loops.
Follows deeply obfuscated binaries recursively enabling tracking logics to completely penetrate into new explicitly spawned sub-threads natively escaping basic tracking vectors.
Modifies logging fundamentally eliminating live streams instead mathematically summing exactly how many times each System Call natively executing outputting clean statistical aggregate distributions.
Prepends standard system clock logic explicitly appending highly explicit live millisecond timestamps cleanly aligned vertically directly next to execution strings.
Forcefully mitigates chaotic visual terminal overload executing massive systemic tracking outputs seamlessly redirecting raw text exclusively into localized filesystem locations.
Modifies standard byte limits forcefully allowing incredibly massive long unencrypted internal string arrays (like massive SQL queries) to inherently print instead of heavily truncating.
Injects native chronological processing logic establishing exact elapsed execution millisecond timings bridged directly between every explicit sequentially intercepted local System Call.
